codeReview.summary: The implementation fully satisfies the validation assertion VAL-MCP-004. McpToolWrapper was modified to store a cached normalized_name field computed in new() as format!('mcp__{}__{}', server.to_lowercase(), tool.to_lowercase()), and the Tool trait name() method now returns &self.normalized_name instead of &self.info.name. A comprehensive test file (crates/swell-tools/tests/mcp_tool_naming.rs) was added covering: TreeSitter+ParseAST→mcp__treesitter__parseast, uppercase normalization, underscore preservation, mixed case, single words, empty server edge case, and registry discovery. All 10 tests pass, 131 MCP tests pass, clippy clean.

summary: PASS — The MCP tool naming convention is correctly implemented. McpToolWrapper::new() now computes normalized_name as format!('mcp__{}__{}', server.to_lowercase(), tool.to_lowercase()) at construction time, caching the result for the lifetime of the wrapper. The Tool trait name() method returns &self.normalized_name. Validation assertion VAL-MCP-004 is satisfied: a server 'TreeSitter' with tool 'ParseAST' is registered as 'mcp__treesitter__parseast'. 10 dedicated tests + 131 MCP tests pass; clippy clean. The worker also included MCP-003 failure classification (McpFailureClass, McpConnectionError) from a prior incomplete worker session, which was correctly committed as part of this work.
